title: ENDPREFIXMAPPING
text: ENDPREFIXMAPPING Operation activated by the SAX parser for each namespace specification in a start tag, when the parser encounters the corresponding end tag. Explain this code operation ENDPREFIXMAPPING params string Prefix: in endparams Implement your code here end; ENDPREFIXMAPPING operation Copy Parameters Prefix —namespace identifier Description When this operation is defined, it is called by PARSE and PARSEFILE when the end tag belonging to a start tag that has an xmlns attribute is encountered. It is called once for each xmlns specification in the start tag, in reverse order, and it is called before the STARTELEMENT operation. For example, given the following XML code: Explain this code <a:y xmlns="http://www.mywebsite.com/default" xmlns:a="http://www.mywebsite.com/a.html" xmlns:b="http://www.mywebsite.com/b.html"> ... </a:y> Copy ENDPREFIXMAPPING is called for each xmlns specification, in the following order: Table 1. Invocation Parameter Values For XML Value of Prefix Comments xmlns:a="http://www.mywebsite.com/b.html" "b" Indicates the end of the scope of prefix b . Subsequent tags in the XML document cannot use this prefix, without redefining it using a xmlns:b= specification. xmlns:a="http://www.mywebsite.com/a.html" "a" Indicates the end of the scope of prefix a . Subsequent tags in the XML document cannot use this prefix, without redefining it using a xmlns:a= specification. xmlns="http://www.mywebsite.com/default" "" Indicates the end of the scope of the default namespace. Subsequent tags without a prefix will not belong to this default namespace. Related Links PARSE Parses a specified XML stream. PARSEFILE Parses an XML document in the specified file. STARTPREFIXMAPPING Operation activated by the SAX parser when it encounters a start tag with one or more xmlns (namespace) specifications. STARTELEMENT Operation activated by the SAX parser when a start tag is encountered. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
